title: Zaluzianskya ovata
taxonomy: Zaluzianskya ovata is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is native to South Africa. The plant belongs to the genus Zaluzianskya, which is a group of fragrant, evening-scented flowers. The classification of Zaluzianskya ovata places it within the order Lamiales. The plant is also known as night-scented Zaluzianskya, referring to its fragrant flowers that bloom at night.

intro: The Zaluzianskya ovata, also known as the night-flowering harebell, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Cape Province in South Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Zaluzianskya ovata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
